elements.sent-2411-038: The most important building-block in Raghavendra's result (as well as in many other PCPs) is the construction of a suitable dictatorship test.
elements.sent-2411-039: A function $f: \Sigma^n \rightarrow \Sigma$ is called a dictatorship function if $f(x)$ depends only on one of $x$'s coordinates.
elements.sent-2411-040: A dictatorship test is a randomized procedure which queries $f$ at a few (correlated) locations randomly and, based on these, decides if $f$ is a dictatorship function or far from any dictator function.
elements.sent-2411-041: There are three important properties of the test which are useful in getting hardness of approximation results for CSPs.
elements.sent-2411-042: The first one is the {\em completeness parameter $c$, which is the probability that the test accepts a dictatorship function.}
elements.sent-2411-043: The second property is the {\em soundness parameter $s$, which is the maximum probability the test accepts a far-from-dictatorship function.}
elements.sent-2411-044: The third property is the decision predicate that the test uses.
elements.sent-2411-045: If the test uses predicates from the class $\mathcal{P}$, has completeness $c$ and soundness $s$, then it can be used to prove a UG-hardness result for Max-$\mathcal{P}$-CSP with completeness $(c-\eps)$ and soundness $(s+\eps)$, for any constant $\eps>0$.

elements.sent-2411-238: If $1$-bounded product functions $P_i: \A_i^n \to \bbC$ satisfy \begin{equation}\left|\E_{(x_1,\dots,x_k) \sim \mu^{\otimes n}} \Big[\prod_{i=1}^k P_i(x_i) \Big] \right| \ge \delta,\end{equation} then for any $\gamma \in (0, 1)$ it holds that \begin{equation}\E_{\substack{x \sim \mu_1^{\otimes n} y \sim_{1-\gamma} x}}[|P_1(x) - P_1(y)|^2] \le O_{\alpha}(\gamma\log(1/\delta)).\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-240: Let $\eta_j$ be such that \begin{equation}\left|\E_{(x_1,\dots,x_k) \sim \mu}\Big[\prod_{i=1}^k P_i^{(j)}(x_i) \Big] \right| = 1 - \eta_j. \label{eq:jcor}\end{equation} Thus, $\prod_{j=1 ^n (1-\eta_j) = \delta$ and hence $\sum_{j=1 ^n \eta_j \le O(\log(1/\delta))$.}}
elements.sent-2411-241: The following equality holds for all $x, y \in \A_1^n$: \begin{equation}\Big|\prod_{j=1}^n P_1^{(j)}(x_j) - \prod_{j=1}^n P_1^{(j)}(y_j) \Big|^2 = ~ \prod_{j=1}^n |P_1^{(j)}(x_j)|^2 + \prod_{j=1}^n |P_1^{(j)}(y_j)|^2 - \prod_{j=1}^n P_1^{(j)}(x_j)\bar{P_1^{(j)}(y_j)} - \prod_{j=1}^n \bar{P_1^{(j)}(x_j)}P_1^{(j)}(y_j).\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-242: Note that for \begin{equation}\E_{x \sim \mu_1, y \sim_{1-\gamma} x} P_1^{(j)}(x)\bar{P_1^{(j)}(y)}\end{equation} is a real number, and thus equals \begin{equation}\E_{x \sim \mu_1, y \sim_{1-\gamma} x} P_1^{(j)}(x)\bar{P_1^{(j)}(y)} = \|P_1^{(j)}\|_2^2 - \frac12 \E_{x \sim \mu_1, y \sim_{1-\gamma} x} |P_1^{(j)}(x) - P_1^{(j)}(y)|^2 \notag \ge \|P_1^{(j)}\|_2^2 - O(\gamma\eta_j) \notag \ge \|P_1^{(j)}\|_2^2(1 - O(\gamma\eta_j)), \label{eq:pjbound}\end{equation} where we used~\cref{lemma:prod1 .}

elements.sent-2411-245: If $1$-bounded function $f: \A_1^n \to \bbC$ and product functions $P_i: \A_i^n \to \bbC$ for $i = 2, \dots, k$ satisfy \begin{equation}\left|\E_{(x_1,\dots,x_k) \sim \mu^{\otimes n}}\Big[f(x_1) \prod_{i=2}^k P_i(x_i) \Big]\right| \ge \delta\end{equation} then for $\gamma \le c\delta^2/\log(1/\delta)$ for sufficiently small $c = c(\alpha)>0$, it holds that $\stab_{1-\gamma (f) \ge \delta^2/4$.}
elements.sent-2411-246: Define the product function $\wt{P : \A_1^n \to \bbC$ as \begin{equation}\wt{P}(x) := \E_{(x_1,\dots,x_k) \sim \mu^{\otimes n} : x_1 = x} \Big[\prod_{i=2}^k P_i(x_i) \Big].\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-247: Then the following inequalities hold: \begin{equation}\delta \le \left|\E_{(x_1,\dots,x_k) \sim \mu^{\otimes n}}\Big[f(x_1) \prod_{i=2}^k P_i(x_i) \Big]\right| = \left|\E_{x \sim \mu_1^{\otimes n}} \Big[f(x)\wt{P}(x) \Big] \right|, \label{eq:wtp}\end{equation} and \begin{equation}\left|\E_{(x_1,\dots,x_k) \sim \mu^{\otimes n}}\Big[\wt{P}(x_1) \prod_{i=2}^k P_i(x_i) \Big]\right| \ge \delta^2.\end{equation}.

elements.sent-2411-249: By \cref{lemma:prodn , we know that for $\gamma \le c\delta^2/\log(1/\delta)$ \begin{equation}\left|\E_{x \sim \mu_1^{\otimes n}, y \sim_{1-\gamma} x}\Big[f(x)(\wt{P}(x) - \wt{P}(y)) \Big] \right| \le \E_{x \sim \mu_1^{\otimes n}, y \sim_{1-\gamma} x}\Big[|\wt{P}(x) - \wt{P}(y)| \Big] \le \E_{x \sim \mu_1^{\otimes n}, y \sim_{1-\gamma} x}\Big[|\wt{P}(x) - \wt{P}(y)|^2 \Big]^{1/2} \le O(\sqrt{\gamma\log(1/\delta)}) \le \delta/2.\end{equation}.}
